message:
feat(actors): actor core abstraction with ActorId + ActorRef<M> (bd-trgk)

This establishes the foundational actor handle types needed for Phase 3 work.

What changed
- Add ActorId as a distinct wrapper over TaskId (arena+generation semantics) to avoid mixing actor identity with generic tasks.
- Add ActorState + ActorStateCell to track Created/Running/Stopping/Stopped without extra allocations.
- Refactor ActorRef from ActorRef<A: Actor> to ActorRef<M> so call sites only depend on message type; add ActorRef::reserve() for the two-phase mailbox protocol and ActorRef::is_alive()/actor_id().
- Teach ActorHandle to carry actor_id + shared state and expose ActorHandle::actor_id(); ensure stop/abort transitions set Stopping for observability.
- Introduce internal ActorCell<M> to centralize actor loop inputs (mailbox, owner region, state, timestamps) and wire lifecycle transitions in run_actor_loop().
- Keep supervised actors stable: only mark Stopped when the outer supervised loop completes (avoids liveness flicker across restarts).

Tests
- Added deterministic unit + lab-runtime E2E tests for ActorId generation, ActorRef cloning/liveness transitions, mailbox drain on cancel, and deterministic replay.
- Verified: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test.
